title: 반했을 때 나타나는 남자의 호감 시그널
text: What are the signs of a man's interest that appear when he falls for someone? I will tell you. Please bear with me until then. This is sentence 1 13 50. How wonderful would it be if we could identify signs of interest just by looking at someone's facial expressions? And is that even possible? To start with the conclusion, I think I can say that it is possible. This is because everyone feels emotions, and their face expresses those emotions like a "Meow index." However, despite this, we actually find it difficult to notice the other person's signs of interest. This is because they appear too quickly or reveal themselves in a subtle way. Therefore, we only sense the signs of interest through the atmosphere. But if we could clearly recognize those signs of interest, the story changes. One thing is certain: we could suffer less pain because of love. So today, I will tell you about and examine the signs of interest a man displays through his face and body language. I am currently filming a collaboration video with *Meeting and Shadow*, and I will use it here to explain things, so please pay attention. First, the easiest place to identify a man's signs of interest is his heels. Our gaze is very honest; we tend to look at what we want to see one more time and completely avoid looking at what we don't want to see. However... The interesting thing is that this characteristic is even more pronounced in the case of this particular man. Since men are visual creatures, they often look at someone they like one more time. In fact, a friend I knew in high school once failed because he stared at a woman he liked over 30 times in a minute. However, 30 times is too much; usually, men just give a quick glance twice like

title: 반했을 때 나타나는 남자의 호감 시그널
text: en look at someone they like one more time. In fact, a friend I knew in high school once failed because he stared at a woman he liked over 30 times in a minute. However, 30 times is too much; usually, men just give a quick glance twice like this. As someone who succeeded once, you were right, and for the second time, you shot water at her. But the important point here is that while men flirt when they meet someone they like, their policy is to quickly look away when their eyes meet. The reason for this is nervousness. Next, there is an instinct to feel a slight fear when seeing an attractive person. This can be explained by evolution. When we went to the West and lived as hunter-gatherers in caves, we lived in small groups. In those small groups, a strong alpha male claimed the majority of attractive women, and most of the men were just average, powerless men. This is where the problem arises. What happens when a powerless, average man hits on an attractive woman? It would be interesting to see him rebel against the strong alpha male. Why? Because he handed over his woman. If things go wrong, he might... They could have been killed or expelled from the group. Back then, being kicked out of the group was tantamount to death. Therefore, for survival, it was more advantageous not to approach attractive members of the opposite sex. That is why that instinct still remains in men's DNA. For this reason, men are actually unable to approach women they consider attractive. That is why, when you make eye contact—you attractive guy—men show signs of avoiding eye contact, particularly by looking down. When they avoid eye contact with a woman, they display a double-faced attitude by constantly glancing at her. The unfortunate thing is that compared to this tendency in men, it is difficult for women to be proactive. Consequently, situations where the relationship ends with mutual satisfaction often result in absurd remarks. Suddenly, bad memories come to mind. Let us remember that only the courageous can win love. At the same time, men smile more in front of someone they like. To be precise, rather than a smile, a flushed expression appears. The corners of the mouth are slightly turned up, and the cheekbones are subtly raised, making it look like they are about to burst into laughter at any moment. In fact, the frequency of laughter increases. At the same time, they start to look foolish. Their words... You might feel a strange disconnect, acting clumsy and unsure of things, as if you're buffering. Why is that? It's because nervousness and trembling cause you to lose the capacity to think. If you go on a date with a guy you're interested in and he acts like an idiot, do n't think, "Is he a bit slow?" Instead, try to think, "He's just nervous because he's so captivated by my charm." So, to summarize: he looks at you more than twice, makes eye contact occasionally, looks away immediately after meeting your gaze, especially when talking; shows a flushed expression; laughs a lot, especially when talking to you; and his behavior and speech are unnatural, as if he's buffering. If you notice these symptoms in a man, I hope you will think about it seriously. That's all for today's psychology session. Likes and subscriptions are a great help. See you next time. Bye.
